stem: A agriculture study measures crop yield across 5 regions, each containing multiple farms. region A has 10 observations with mean 69.2 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 6.5). region B has 5 observations with mean 70.9 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 4.4). region C has 5 observations with mean 63.5 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 6.4). region D has 8 observations with mean 46.5 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 9.6). region E has 15 observations with mean 64.4 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 8.1). The between-region standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 3.1 and 7.8 tonnes/hectare. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true crop yield for region D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-region variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for region D: 51.4 tonnes/hectare (shrunk from raw 46.5). Range [49.1, 55.4] as between-region SD varies from 3.1 to 7.8. Pooled mean: 62.8.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 62.8 tonnes/hectare (weighted average of 5 region means).
Shrinkage factor for region D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/8). At σ_between=5.2: B = 0.701.
Interval [49.1, 55.4] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (46.5) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (62.8) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
